creator: rempas@tutanota.com
text:
(In reply to Vladimir Druzenko from comment #5)

Ok, I have good news and bad news!

The good news is that, trying another USB 3 port, works! I wasn't using UBS 3 ports because when I did, my Linux OS has problems and sometimes, it would also "freeze" in the BIOS booting screen (I think it's called "POST" or something). But I did think of trying it anyway, and it does work.

Bad news, is that my installation is broken. I did try to install it two more times, and it doesn't work. I did use a manual partitioning in an MBR disk and I couldn't seem to make it work. The installation guide in the Handbook is outdated. There is no "label" and, you first need to create partitions as just "freebsd" and not mount them anywhere and then, create a "nested" partition with the desired filesystem. I seem to did everything, but I couldn't make it work...  I will try again at some point when I find the time and motivation to do so.

Thank you so much for the help and I wish you to have an amazing day!
